plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-531 RP17.P04.M06.S03-RP17.P05.M02.S14 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-531 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP17 p04 closeout p05 foundation: the P04 Synthetic Fixture Set tail (the twenty remaining rows), the full P04 Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet, and Closeout And Next Handoff cycles, the full P05 Scope Inventory and Contract Draft cycles, and the P05 Type And Shape Definition head (the first fourteen rows) as eight descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H17), Claude review packet (C17), and CP00-532 handoff, without opening AI governance, model policy, retrieval scope, or citation runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-531 is a descriptor-only RP17 AI Governance closeout pack (Risk C, 150 units, RP17.P04.M06.S03-RP17.P05.M02.S14). The pack-level production_ready:false claim is supported and correct: implementation_layer is "descriptor", runtime_ready is false, and every runtime flag in service.js freezeCp531Result is false (writes_product_state, creates/updates_database_rows, reads/writes_object_storage, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, executes_api_handler/ui_runtime/command_runtime, writes_permission_decision, appends_audit_trace, loads_real_fixture_data), with no_real_data:true and synthetic_only:true. Internal consistency is exact across review-baseline, registry pack binding/requirements, contract current_pack + p04_closeout_p05_foundation_descriptor, plan pack, and manifest: deliverable distribution {ui:43,claude_review:13,security_audit:14,implementation:41,fixture:25,test:8,hermes_evidence:6}=150 and the 8 micro-title row counts=150 all match. validators.js comprehensively checks coverage/section/descriptor-only/no-leak guards and rejects Claude-final-approval and enterprise-trust-from-local-validation; model.test.js exercises these against the real plan and contract JSON; the contract validator imports via the package index (proving CP531 exports resolve) and deepEquals the contract against code structures. The ai-governance source is IO-free (only internal registry.js imports; no fs/network/db). Authority boundary holds: Claude read_only:true and claude_is_final_approval:false everywhere, human final approval remains required outside this receipt, and Hermes is used only for the deterministic evidence-packet descriptor (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). CP178+ review hardening is encoded: forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failure, zero-byte output, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, and missing references; the readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1434B stdout) and the validation-report shows no forbidden tools, prompt within size limits, schema present, and source_inspection_mode read_tools_used. No premature CP532 implementation exists, and unrelated dirty files (control-room, workbook, ldip) are correctly excluded from pack scope. Scope caveat: the 27 attested command-evidence results (all exit 0) and the H17 gate cannot be re-executed under read-only review; I verified the implementation is fully consistent with those claims rather than re-running them. One P3 informational observation only; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Manifest evidence_refs forward-reference post-review artifacts not present at review time
findings.details: Each unit's evidence_refs (e.g. RP17.P04.M06.S06 at lines 1671-1677, repeated across all 150 units) lists claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-531/. These files do not exist yet (Glob of docs/closeout-packs/cp00-531/** returns only manifest.json and command-evidence.json). This is expected and by-design: in AI_GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP514_REQUIREMENTS.hardened_review_sequence those artifacts are produced in the receipt_normalization, finding_adjudication, and construction_inspection steps that run AFTER this read_only Claude run, so they are necessarily absent during the review itself. Noting it so a downstream verifier inspecting the manifest in isolation does not treat the dangling references as a defect.
findings.recommendation: No code change required. Optionally, the closeout tooling could populate/verify these evidence_refs after the adjudication and construction-inspection steps complete (before commit/final_closeout_validation) so the committed manifest's references resolve.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected per hardened_review_sequence; non-blocking. Verified the referenced artifacts are produced post-review (after the Claude run) and that all in-scope source, contract, plan, test, and validator artifacts are present and consistent.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-531 ai governance core p04 closeout p05 foundation descriptor pack
